title: ED eye injury documentation

presenting_history.text: Supports minor-injury, foreign-body and contact-lens context plus pain, blepharospasm, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact corneal-abrasion features section supports onset, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, risk context and injury mechanism for a possible superficial corneal injury; it does not diagnose an abrasion or cover every eye injury.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports minor-injury, foreign-body and contact-lens context plus pain, blepharospasm, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.

exact_section.heading: Clinical information and patient summary — causes and symptoms

red_flags.text: Supports clinician assessment of the involved area, fluorescein documentation, and referral when injury is deeper, contaminated, or possibly infected; medicine details are not mapped.
red_flags.rationale: The exact assessment and referral section supports recording focused examination, appearance, time-critical features and specialist discussion or escalation only when assessed or clinician-decided.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician assessment of the involved area, fluorescein documentation, and referral when injury is deeper, contaminated, or possibly infected; medicine details are not mapped.

red_flags.text: Lists severe chemical burns, penetrating injuries, hyphema, embedded corneal foreign body, corneal or lid laceration, blunt trauma, severe abrasion, infective keratitis, and other urgent anterior-eye presentations.
red_flags.rationale: The exact anterior-eye and trauma referral table supports documenting traumatic mechanism, urgent features and arranged handover only when identified and acted on by the clinician.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists severe chemical burns, penetrating injuries, hyphema, embedded corneal foreign body, corneal or lid laceration, blunt trauma, severe abrasion, infective keratitis, and other urgent anterior-eye presentations.

source.title: Guidance for Professional Practice Annex 4: Urgency of referrals table

exact_section.heading: Annex 4 — anterior eye and traumatic red-eye referrals

red_flags.text: Lists sudden visual loss, sudden vision change, flashes or floaters with retinal signs, retinal detachment, acute diplopia, severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, pain on eye movement, and new pupil defects.
red_flags.rationale: Vision loss, severe pain and other urgent eye presentations were reviewed as clinician assessment prompts, never as conclusions.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists sudden visual loss, sudden vision change, flashes or floaters with retinal signs, retinal detachment, acute diplopia, severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, pain on eye movement, and new pupil defects.

source.title: Guidance for Professional Practice Annex 4: Urgency of referrals table

exact_section.heading: Annex 4 — visual loss and other urgent presentations

source_limitations: Visual acuity details, laterality, contact lens, chemical exposure, penetrating mechanism, globe integrity, pupil, fluorescein, intraocular pressure, eyelid injury, diagnosis, treatment, procedure and exact referral urgency remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
